mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 9.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect vanishes (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression models, or 60d returns in high-demand regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening cycle characterized by rising federal funds rates and declining money supply (RSAFS) compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns during the 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 0.25% and RSAFS declines > 1% in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20d returns in high-industrial-production regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k per month signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 1.5% signals deflationary risks that reduce corporate pricing power, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60d returns in low-inflation regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below 90 combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) above 9.5% indicates a recessionary risk that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect vanishes (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression models, or 20d returns in recessionary regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
